headline: Model disagreement on market direction amid geopolitical tensions and tech catalysts
summary: Five AI models made 15 predictions across major indices and tech stocks on 2026-04-09, with sharply divergent views on market direction. Claude focused on geopolitical risk (Iran ceasefire breakdown, oil surge) to predict declines in SPY/QQQ but gains in energy, while GPT-4o, Grok, and others emphasized tech earnings strength and bullish sentiment. Energy (XLE) emerged as consensus bullish play, but fundamental disagreement persisted on broad market direction with no outcomes available yet.

consensus_picks.note: Significant model divergence: Claude and Perplexity predicted down (0.75/0.6 confidence) while GPT-4o and Grok predicted up (0.65 confidence each).
Claude and Perplexity bearish on tech amid stagflation fears; GPT-4o bullish on earnings strength with conflicting confidence levels.
Strong consensus on energy upside (0.8/0.7 confidence) driven by oil surge to $101+ on geopolitical tensions and Strait of Hormuz concerns.

best_call.summary: Claude's 0.72 confidence META buy on $21B CoreWeave AI infrastructure deal was backed by concrete fundamental catalyst with premarket momentum, representing the most catalytic bullish thesis of the day.

worst_call.summary: GPT-4o's QQQ target of 400.0 appears implausibly low (current trading ~600+) suggesting a data error or miscalibration, severely undermining credibility of their tech bullish thesis despite correct directional call.
